channel.title: NYT > New York
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Avila Chevalier Won Despite Far-Left Views. Will They Hurt Democrats?
item.description: Darializa Avila Chevalier’s questionable social media history and outlier politics are already being highlighted by Republican leaders as they look to November.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg
item.media:credit: Lexi Parra/The New York Times
item.media:description: Darializa Avila Chevalier was one of three House candidates, all backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who won primary battles on Tuesday.

item.title: Queens State Assembly Candidate Charged With Forgery for A.I. Use
item.description: The arraignment on Wednesday is said to be the first time a politician in New York is facing criminal charges relating to A.I. interference in an election.
item.dc:creator: Miles G. Cohen
item.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
item.media:description: A prosecutor accused Jonathan Rinaldi of using artificial intelligence to “advance the selfish goal of winning a City Council seat by any means.”

item.title: Why New York’s Democratic Establishment Fell to Team Mamdani
item.description: Democratic leaders found that their traditional tools, from rallies to phone banks to big-name campaign events, were no match for the left’s ground game and messaging.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos and Dana Rubinstein
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Adriano Espaillat lost his re-election bid despite a late-stage campaign visit from Hakeem Jeffries, the Democratic House leader.

item.title: Judge Orders U.S. to Stop Seeking Medical Records of Transgender Youth
item.description: The Justice Department had sought information about care provided at New York City hospitals, which were the subject of the ruling.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein
item.category: New York University Langone Medical Center
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie/The New York Times
item.media:description: NYU Langone, a major Manhattan medical system, had received a subpoena seeking identifying information and medical records about adolescent patients who had received transition care.

item.title: After Bruising Primaries in New York, Democrats Look to November Elections
item.description: The party secured key victories in New York City, but battles across the state suggest a larger fight awaits in the fall.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cait Conley, right, will face Representative Mike Lawler in what may be the Democratic Party’s best chances of flipping a House seat.

item.title: The Biggest Winner: Zohran Mamdani
item.description: All the candidates the mayor backed won their primary races on Tuesday, enhancing his political clout in the city and the state.
item.dc:creator: James Barron
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times

item.title: Frank Carone, Longtime Eric Adams Associate, Is Arrested in Bribery Scheme
item.description: Brooklyn federal prosecutors accuse Mr. Carone and his brother of profiting from the city’s migrant crisis. He was the chief of staff to former Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Michael Rothfeld, Dana Rubinstein and Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
item.media:description: Frank Carone, former chief of staff to Mayor Eric Adams of New York, was taken into custody along with three others on federal corruption charges.

item.title: New Clues Point to Taylor Swift Wedding Event at M.S.G.
item.description: The New York Times has confirmed that Taylor Swift has rented the sports arena for the July 4 weekend, suggesting plans for a days-long celebration.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman, Jonah E. Bromwich, Emma G. Fitzsimmons, Dana Rubinstein and Maria Cramer
item.media:credit: Gregory Shamus/Getty Images
item.media:description: There has been intense speculation about the details of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding ever since they announced their engagement in August 2025.

item.title: Mamdani’s Victories May Be a Headache for the Governor Who Has Backed Him
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul has worked with Mayor Zohran Mamdani on child care and second-home taxes. But the triumphs of democratic socialists on Primary Day could complicate her vision.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Shannon Stapleton/Reuters
item.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul celebrated the victory of Micah Lasher, an establishment candidate.

item.title: After Banner Night, D.S.A. Sharpens Its Policy and Political Ambitions
item.description: After democratic socialists captured two House primaries and several state legislative contests in New York, the group’s leaders mulled how to use their expanding influence.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Jeffery C. Mays
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally to push the Tax the Rich movement was held in Albany earlier this year, but Mayor Zohran Mamdani did not attend.

item.title: Trump Administration and the M.T.A. Clash Over Penn Station Redesign
item.description: President Trump has made revitalizing the cramped train hall a priority, but he will need the cooperation of a transit agency his administration has long criticized.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: Today’s Pennsylvania Station is often regarded with contempt by commuters.

item.title: Why These Affordable Homes Face a 31% Rent Increase
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has promised relief for rent-stabilized tenants. But thousands of apartments under a separate city program are subject to increases, creating an awkward contrast.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri and Dana Rubinstein
item.category: Department of Housing Preservation and Development (NYC)
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tracey Towers in the Bronx is part of the Mitchell-Lama program, which created a decades-old bastion of middle-income and working-class housing in New York City.

item.title: Mamdani’s Rent Freeze Could Become Reality Today. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: A freeze for rent-stabilized homes is expected when a city panel votes on Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s plan on Thursday, but the move is likely to face legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri
item.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s pledge to “freeze the rent” for rent-stabilized apartments was a popular rallying cry during his mayoral campaign.

item.title: Mamdani Ends Day Care Contract With Eric Adams’s Former Romantic Partner
item.description: The city had accused Jasmine Ray of fiscal mismanagement. She said that she had been unfairly targeted because of her ties to the former mayor.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag
item.category: Mayor's Office of Sports, Wellness and Recreation (NYC)
item.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Cornerstone Day Care Center, which opened in 1966, is among the oldest day cares in Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn.

item.title: Representative Tom Kean, Missing for Months, Is Back Home in New Jersey
item.description: The congressman, who has been absent from Washington since March, answered the door of his home on Wednesday evening. He was wearing a suit and tie.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr., absent from Congress for nearly four months, was back at his New Jersey home on Wednesday evening.

item.title: What Does a Knicks Trash Can Cost? $168, or for One Woman, Her Job.
item.description: After a video of Angie Baez taking a Knicks-themed trash can went viral, a JPMorgan Chase spokeswoman said she was no longer with the company.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman and Alexandra E. Petri
item.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
item.media:description: The New York Knicks’ championship parade last week in Manhattan.

item.title: In New York Election Results, More Evidence of Eroding Support for Israel
item.description: If the shift in public opinion continues, it could reshape one of the United States’ closest alliances.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Jennifer Medina
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York campaigning with Claire Valdez. “We will stand up to the genocide,” Ms. Valdez told supporters after winning her Democratic House primary on Tuesday.

item.title: NY Jewish Leaders Wrestle With Israel Critics’ Sweep in Primary Elections
item.description: Pro-Israel leaders in New York expressed alarm at the primary victories of three Democratic candidates backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Progressive Jewish groups celebrated.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: ‘Henry VI,’ ‘Camping’ and 6 More Shows to See Now
item.description: New York in June is a bonanza of Off and Off Off Broadway productions. Here’s our guide to shows we recommend, many in the city’s coziest spaces (and closing soon).
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Calling Democrats ‘Communists,’ Trump Keeps Close Eye on N.Y. Primaries
item.description: The president posted repeatedly into Wednesday morning to label Democrats as extreme, delight in his rivals’ losses and express some apparent envy of Mayor Zohran Mamdani.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has developed a relatively friendly rapport with Mayor Zohran Mamdani, despite their vast differences.

item.title: N.Y.P.D., F.B.I. Search Homes of Police Officials in Corruption Inquiries
item.description: The searches, by police internal affairs investigators and federal authorities, stemmed from earlier scrutiny of Jeffrey Maddrey, the former chief of the department, according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Chelsia Rose Marcius, Maria Cramer and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:credit: Heather Khalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Investigators searched the homes of current and former New York Police Department officials, including at this address on Manhattan’s Upper East Side.

item.title: Homes for Sale in New York and New Jersey
item.description: This week’s properties are a four-bedroom in Metuchen, N.J., and a three-bedroom in Bronxville, N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Jill P. Capuzzo and Anne Mancuso

item.title: Homes for Sale in Manhattan and Brooklyn
item.description: This week’s properties are in the East Village, East Harlem and Crown Heights.
item.dc:creator: Heather Senison

item.title: What’s Behind Broadway’s New Musical Drought?
item.description: Hollywood actors in starry plays, skittish investors and gate-keeping theater owners have all contributed to an unusually tough climate for song-and-dance shows.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.category: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button (Play)
Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil (Play)
item.media:credit: Tim Lahan
